data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer) vs WHOLE roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes (high-confidence + benign-reading filter); all CLEAN, verified against code:

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state (parent What/Scope). Confirmed via search: `managed_refs` has zero matches in the tree → parent builds it; this child only READS the gate/set. Reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent ⇒ REMOVE/CLEAR iff ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, no inbound-remove dual. ba2c ("CLEAR only when Jira parent ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT") matches 908c ("REMOVE iff ∈ managed_refs, else adopt") and parent's `should_propagate_removal` definition. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap — ba2c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate existing; the parent-child hierarchy (both children carry parent_id b4d4, the primitive builder) supplies ordering, and parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. No undeclared gap. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap — ba2c touches the PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`, the `emit_detach_clear` branch; verified outbound_differ.py:400-414, `result["parent"] = None`); sibling 908c touches the LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE). Disjoint code paths confirmed. Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic (ba2c AC4: "no duplicated provenance logic"). CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation — ba2c and 908c are independent consumers (parent vs links); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact — both children extend same-pass inbound suppression, but on DISJOINT keys: ba2c uses the scalar `fields`/`parent`→`parent_id` path (_OUTBOUND_TO_INBOUND_FIELD, inbound_differ.py:578-581,633-644); 908c uses the link-add echo set `link_add_keys` (inbound_differ.py:599-601,619-623). Verified disjoint. No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references — ba2c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog" in its Design; confirmed b4d4's alias IS safe-luge-nog and 908c cites the same. ba2c's own alias is tan-elbow-mica. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

NON-G4 note (within-child / G2-G6-class, NOT a cross-child contradiction with ba2c): the Jira "Blocks" type collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` logical kinds direction-agnostically, while parent `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind, target)`. This affects sibling 908c's link-REMOVE gate lookup, is already flagged on 908c and absorbed as a parent G6 finding, and does NOT contradict ba2c (parent ref is single-direction `(parent, target)`, unambiguous). Not a cross-child interaction defect.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).
